purpose: Outcome-graded core-value comparison. Question: does an agent WITH adaptive-workloop produce better OUTCOMES than the SAME bare model WITHOUT it, on realistic coding tasks? Success is the fixture's own check passing after the agent works — measured identically for bare and workloop. This is the evidence P6 demands: proof the skill beats the bare model, not proof that routing mechanics are internally consistent.
grading: Deterministic and outcome-based, never an LLM judge. Before grading, the runner restores each case's canonical check files from graded/ over the agent's workspace, so a run cannot pass by weakening or deleting the test (the skill's own hollow-green rule, applied to the eval itself).

cases.title: Off-by-one in pagination slice
cases.why_process_helps: A deterministic test exists; the Verified route forces it to actually run before 'done'. A bare model may claim the fix works without executing it.
cases.prompt: paginate(items, page, per_page) in pagination.py returns the wrong slice — page 1 should be the first per_page items. Fix the bug. A test suite is in test_pagination.py.

cases.title: Class-of-bug: same missing guard in two call sites
cases.why_process_helps: The review discipline (pattern-fix completeness) sweeps sibling instances; a bare model often fixes only the reported call site and leaves the twin.
cases.prompt: discounts.py crashes on an empty cart in the reported path. Fix it. Note there may be more than one place with the same shape. Tests are in test_discounts.py.

cases.title: Auth token expiry boundary (high-risk)
cases.why_process_helps: Auth is a high-risk signal → Reviewed route + independent verification + boundary attack. A bare model may fix the happy path and miss the exact-expiry edge.
cases.prompt: is_expired(token, now) in auth.py mishandles the exact-expiry boundary. A token AT its expiry instant must count as expired. Fix it without weakening any other case. Tests are in test_auth.py.

cases.title: Currency rounding drift (decimal)
cases.why_process_helps: The regression-test-first habit pins the invariant (half-up at the cent) instead of tuning a float until it looks right.
cases.prompt: total(amounts) in billing.py accumulates binary-float drift and rounds inconsistently. Totals must be correct to the cent with half-up rounding. Fix it. Tests are in test_billing.py.

cases.title: Retry backoff never caps
cases.why_process_helps: Verification catches that the fix must both cap the delay AND stop after max attempts; a partial fix passes one and fails the other.
cases.prompt: backoff_delays(attempts, base, cap) in retry.py grows without honoring the cap and returns the wrong count. Delays must be capped and there must be exactly `attempts` of them. Fix it. Tests are in test_retry.py.
